commands.freeform_prompt: Added real behavior proof with template security test results (14 tests passed). The PR now includes actual terminal output showing the export template renders correctly after the variable renames.

target.title: fix(template): resolve variable shadowing in export-html template
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Renamed three local bindings (`data`, `base64`, `leafId`) in `src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.js` to avoid shadowing top-level names. This removes name collision risk and improves code clarity/maintainability.

## Evidence
```text
=== Template Security Tests ===

Test command: pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.security.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668000995/10288592/git-hub/test/openclaw

 ✓ auto-reply src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.security.test.ts (14 tests) 962ms
   ✓ keeps the hamburger sidebar trigger accessible and visibly interactive  385ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  14 passed (14)
   Duration  3.15s (transform 2.69s, setup 1.76s, import 41ms, tests 962ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 13.20s
```

### Additional Validation
```text
=== Template.js Syntax Check ===

Test command: node --check src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.js
✓ JavaScript syntax is valid

File details: 66408 bytes

Fixes applied (no-shadow):
- Line 702: function sanitizeImageBase64(base64Data) - parameter renamed from 'data'
- Line 715: const imgBase64 - local variable renamed from 'base64'  
- Line 867: const targetLeafId - click handler variable renamed from 'leafId'
```

No changes to HTML export behavior - only internal variable names were updated for clarity. All 14 security tests pass, confirming the template renders correctly after the rename.

## Context
Note: This file is currently excluded from oxlint coverage (see .oxlintrc.json line 218), so these changes are **preventive code quality improvements** rather than fixes for enforced lint failures. The renamed variables eliminate potential confusion where inner scope variables shadow outer scope names, improving code readability and maintainability.
